subject: Cloud Computing
title: Bulkhead Isolation
description: A quick win: understand Bulkhead Isolation.

hook.text: A ship has watertight compartments. If one floods, the rest stay dry. Cloud systems need the same design.

buildup.text: Bulkhead isolation separates components so a failure in one doesn't bring down the others.

discovery.text: In practice: separate thread pools, separate databases, and separate scaling for critical vs. non-critical services.

twist.text: Isolation costs more. Running separate resources for each component means paying for duplicate infrastructure.

climax.text: The trade-off is worth it. When a non-critical feature fails, your payments and core services keep running.

punchline.text: Isolate the critical from the optional. Let non-essentials fail alone.

quiz.question: What does bulkhead isolation prevent in cloud systems?
options: A failure in one component from cascading and bringing down the entire system
Users from accessing services during maintenance windows
Data from being replicated across multiple regions
